methods.name: Stage-Progression
methods.rationale: Applies typical Series A → next-stage step-up multiplier (2.0x) to $60M total raised proxy.

methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.skip_reason: insufficient narrow peer set in Nuclear Energy (found 1 same-stage, 1 adjacent-stage, 0 similar-tier; need ≥3)
methods.rationale: 8 same-sector peers exist in Nuclear Energy, but none of the narrow filters (same-stage / adjacent-stage / similar-valuation-tier) yielded ≥3 datapoints. Falling back to all-sector median would be misleading.

private.name: Fuse Energy
private.rationale: Same sector (Nuclear Energy), same stage (Series A), post-money $200M+

data.narrative:
Renaissance Fusion is a Series A nuclear energy company founded by Francesco Volpe and Martin Kupp that has raised $60.5M to date. The nuclear energy sector comp universe includes 3 public companies (OKLO, NNE, SMR) and 8 private peers including Fuse Energy, TerraPower, X-Energy, Kairos Power, and Oklo.

Stage-progression methodology is applicable and yields a $121M implied valuation, applying a 2.0x multiplier typical of Series A advancement to the $60.5M capital raised proxy. The peer-median approach is not applicable due to insufficient narrow-set peers; while 8 same-sector comparables exist, none satisfy minimum clustering requirements (same-stage, adjacent-stage, or similar valuation tier) at the necessary threshold of 3+ datapoints. A broad sector median would risk misalignment given stage and scale heterogeneity within nuclear energy financings.

The analysis supports a valuation range of $103M (low) to $139M (high) centered on $121M median. This single-method triangulation reflects a binding constraint: the nuclear energy financing landscape remains sparse at Renaissance Fusion's specific maturity level. Key uncertainties include capital intensity trajectory post-Series A, regulatory pathway risk, and comparable company selection as the sector scales. Additional conviction would require either emergence of closer-stage peer valuations or progression to Series B financing clarity.
